annotations.label: NP-hard (informal)

anchor.snippet: is called NP-hard if, for every problem

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize computational complexity classes; there is no notion of NP-hardness.

annotations.label: Subset sum is NP-hard

anchor.snippet: A simple example of an NP-hard problem is the subset sum problem

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Combinatorics/Additive/SubsetSum.lean` concerns additive combinatorics, not the NP-hard decision problem.

annotations.label: NP-hard at least as difficult as NP

anchor.snippet: Informally, if H is NP-hard, then it is at least as difficult to solve

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of polynomial-time reductions or NP-hardness.

annotations.label: NP-hard via many-one reduction

anchor.snippet: A decision problem H is NP-hard when for every problem L in NP

annotations.note: Polynomial-time many-one reductions and NP are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-hard via NP-complete reduction

anchor.snippet: Another definition is to require that there be a polynomial-time reduction

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks any formalization of NP-completeness or polynomial-time reductions.

annotations.label: NP-hard not in P if P≠NP

anchor.snippet: If P ≠ NP, then NP-hard problems could not be solved in polynomial time

annotations.note: Classes P and NP are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Approximability of NP-hard optimization

anchor.snippet: Some NP-hard optimization problems can be polynomial-time approximated

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize approximation algorithms or NP-hard optimization problems.

annotations.label: NP-complete problems are NP-hard

anchor.snippet: All NP-complete problems are also NP-hard

annotations.note: Neither NP-complete nor NP-hard are defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Travelling salesman is NP-hard

anchor.snippet: commonly known as the travelling salesman problem

annotations.note: The travelling salesman problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subset sum decision problem

anchor.snippet: The subset sum problem is another example

annotations.note: The subset sum decision problem is not formalized as an NP-hardness example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Halting problem is NP-hard not NP-complete

anchor.snippet: There are decision problems that are NP-hard but not NP-complete such as the halting problem

annotations.note: The halting problem's undecidability is formalized as `halting_problem`, but Mathlib does not state its NP-hardness or non-NP-completeness.

annotations.label: True quantified Boolean formulas

anchor.snippet: the language of true quantified Boolean formulas is decidable in polynomial space

annotations.note: PSPACE and QBF are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP class

anchor.snippet: Class of computational decision problems for which any given

annotations.note: The complexity class NP is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-hard class

anchor.snippet: Class of problems which are at least as hard as the hardest problems in NP

annotations.note: NP-hardness is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-complete class

anchor.snippet: Class of decision problems which contains the hardest problems in NP

annotations.note: NP-completeness is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-easy class

anchor.snippet: At most as hard as NP, but not necessarily in NP

annotations.note: NP-easy is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-equivalent class

anchor.snippet: Decision problems that are both NP-hard and NP-easy

annotations.note: NP-equivalent is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-intermediate existence

anchor.snippet: If P and NP are different, then there exist decision problems in the region of NP

annotations.note: Ladner's theorem and NP-intermediate problems are not formalized in Mathlib.
